target.description: Local MCP server launched over stdio.

tools.name: ra_send
tools.description: SENSITIVE: Send a payment. TWO-STEP PROCESS REQUIRED: Step 1: Call with user_confirmed=false to get fee preview (NO payment is made). Step 2: Show the fee_breakdown from the response to the user and ask them to confirm. Step 3: ONLY after user explicitly approves the fee, call again with user_confirmed=true. A 2% Ra Pay application fee applies. The preview shows the Ra Pay application fee before any money moves. COMPLIANCE SCREENING REQUIRED: Before processing, you MUST verify the business_purpose does NOT contain: (1) P2P patterns: friend, family, roommate, reimbursement for personal expenses, splitting bills, payback, loan, gift, birthday, holiday (2) Money laundering red flags: gift cards, prepaid cards, cryptocurrency purchase, wire transfer (3) Vague purposes: 'for services', 'payment', 'transfer' without specifics (4) Gibberish: repetitive characters (aaaa), random letters (adlkfjal), or repeated words (test test test). If detected, WARN the user that Ra Pay is for business transactions only and ask them to provide a legitimate business purpose.

amount.description: Amount to send in USD cents (e.g., 10000 = $100.00). Minimum: 100 cents ($1.00)

recipient_id.description: Stripe connected account ID (format: acct_xxx). Must be a valid Stripe account.

business_purpose.description: Business purpose for this payment (10-200 characters). Required for compliance. Must describe actual goods or services rendered (e.g., 'Logo design work', 'API consulting'). REJECT purposes containing: friend, family, roommate, reimbursement, loan, gift, birthday, gift card, prepaid card, splitting bills, payback, personal expense, crypto, bitcoin. ALSO REJECT gibberish: repetitive chars (aaaa), random letters, repeated words (test test test). Ra Pay is a B2B payment platform - NOT for peer-to-peer transfers. Valid examples: 'Freelance development work - Invoice #123', 'API consulting services - March 2026'. Invalid examples: 'Lunch reimbursement', 'Gift for friend', 'Gift cards for team', 'aaaaaaaaaa', 'asdfghjkl'.

user_confirmed.description: REQUIRED. Must be false on first call to get fee preview. Only set to true AFTER showing user the fee_breakdown and getting their explicit approval.

tools.name: ra_refund
tools.description: SENSITIVE: Open Stripe Dashboard to process refunds. For security, refunds are processed through the Stripe Dashboard interface. This opens your browser to the refund management page.

tools.name: ra_balance
tools.description: Check available balance in your Ra Pay account. Returns current balance, pending amounts, and payout schedule.

tools.name: ra_history
tools.description: Get transaction history from your Ra Pay account. Returns recent payments, subscriptions, and refunds with timestamps and status.

limit.description: Number of transactions to retrieve (default: 10, max: 100)

tools.name: ra_whoami
tools.description: Check account status and identity. Returns user ID, Stripe account status, verification status, and account tier.

tools.name: ra_dashboard
tools.description: Open the Stripe Dashboard in the user's browser. Provides access to the full Stripe account overview including payments, payouts, and settings.

tools.name: ra_dispute
tools.description: Open the Stripe Disputes page in the user's browser. Disputes and chargebacks must be managed through the Stripe Dashboard interface. Respond to disputes within the timeframe to avoid auto-loss.

scorecard.why_this_score: No deterministic issues were detected in the current server surface.
scorecard.score_meaning: Deterministic CI-first quality scorecard based on conformance, security-relevant capabilities, ergonomics, and metadata hygiene.
limitations: Low score means more deterministic findings or higher-risk exposed surface, not malicious intent.
High score means fewer deterministic findings, not a guarantee of safety.

checks.title: Duplicate tool names
checks.rationale: Tool names should be unique within one MCP server.

checks.title: Missing tool description
checks.rationale: Each tool should include a non-empty description.

checks.title: Overly generic tool name
checks.rationale: Tool names should communicate behavior clearly.

checks.title: Vague tool description
checks.rationale: Tool descriptions should clearly explain what the tool does.

checks.title: Missing schema type
checks.rationale: Tool input schemas should declare a top-level type.

checks.title: Schema allows arbitrary properties
checks.rationale: Tool input schemas should not allow arbitrary top-level properties.

checks.title: Weak input schema
checks.rationale: Tool input schemas should constrain free-form payloads clearly.

checks.title: Missing required critical fields
checks.rationale: Critical schema fields such as path, command, or URL should be required.

checks.title: Dangerous execution tool
checks.rationale: Tools that execute host shell commands are high risk.

checks.title: Dangerous download-and-execute tool
checks.rationale: Tools that combine remote download with shell execution are high risk.

checks.title: Dangerous filesystem write tool
checks.rationale: Tools that write files on disk are high risk.

checks.title: Dangerous filesystem delete tool
checks.rationale: Tools that delete files or directories are high risk.

checks.title: Dangerous HTTP request tool
checks.rationale: Tools that issue arbitrary HTTP requests are high risk.

checks.title: Dangerous network tool
checks.rationale: Tools that expose generic network access are high risk.

checks.title: Write tool without scope hint
checks.rationale: Filesystem mutation tools should document scope constraints clearly.

checks.title: Description mentions destructive access
checks.rationale: Tool descriptions should make destructive broad-scope access easy to spot.
